decision: Special Town Meeting of the Town of Millbrook — Article 4: appropriation of the net proceeds ($12,000,000) from the sale of the Harrow Mill upland campus
reason: The June 2026 sale of the 38-acre upland mill campus to Ridgeline Logistics netted the town a one-time $12M. Under the town charter, any single appropriation over $500,000 outside the annual budget must be decided by a Special Town Meeting of the registered voters.

meeting.note: The only room in town that holds everybody; overflow seating in the cafeteria with a live audio feed run by the AV club

moderator: Elected Town Moderator (third term), who owns one gavel, one stopwatch, and the room

quorum.rule: 250 registered voters checked in by the call to order; if quorum fails, the meeting adjourns to November 10 and the article carries over

who_may_vote.eligible: Registered voters of the Town of Millbrook, both precincts (Millbrook Village and Harlow Crossing), present in the hall
who_may_vote.non_resident_property_owners: May be recognized to speak at the moderator's discretion; may not vote
who_may_vote.minors_and_visitors: Seated in the bleacher section; the PTO runs child care in the school library

ballot_procedure.default: Voice vote on Article 4, called by the moderator
ballot_procedure.secret_ballot: A written petition of 25 registered voters, filed before the vote, requires a secret paper ballot with checklist and ballot box; such a petition is universally expected
ballot_procedure.amendments: Amendments from the floor (including splitting the appropriation) are in order if germane to Article 4, at the moderator's ruling
ballot_procedure.threshold: Simple majority of voters present and voting

absentee_provisions.general_rule: Open town meeting requires presence; there is no general absentee ballot
absentee_provisions.homebound_pilot: Under the state's Municipal Modernization Act pilot, homebound residents certified by the town nurse may cast a supervised early ballot on Article 4 only, at home, during the week before the meeting; 31 residents qualified as of October
absentee_provisions.deployed_service_members: May file a written statement to be read into the record; may not vote by proxy

registration.where: Town Clerk's office, Millbrook Town Hall, or at the DMV counter in the county seat

warrant.article_4_text: To see if the Town will vote to appropriate the net proceeds of the Harrow Mill land sale, being $12,000,000, to fund one of the following: (a) the Riverwalk Greenway project; (b) the Fix It First roads, bridge, and drainage program; or (c) retirement of outstanding bonded debt and unfunded pension liability; or take any other action relative thereto.

traditions.cider: The fire company auxiliary serves hot cider in the lobby; donations fund the pancake breakfast
traditions.bake_sale: Harlow Elementary PTO bake sale, gym entrance — historically the true measure of turnout
traditions.seating: Nobody assigns sides, and yet the Village sits left of the center aisle and the Crossing sits right, as it has been since anyone can remember
